---
title: "PFAS Project 2025"
author: "Doris Mbitazi Asongafac"
date: "2025-07-07"
output: pdf_document
---

##PROJECT: Predicting PFOA Concentration in Groundwater Wells in 32 States in the US: A Machine Learning Approach Socioeconomically Assessment
```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(janitor)      # For cleaning column names
library(dplyr)        # For data manipulation
library(lubridate)    # For date handling
library(tidyr)
library(forcats)

```


```{r}
# DATA LOADING
#Set working directory
setwd("C:/Users/doris/Downloads/Praticum II/pfas project")

#Load the data
env <- read.csv("env.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
wells <- read.csv("wells.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

#Check the dimensions of the datasets
cat("env dataset:", nrow(env), "rows,", ncol(env), "columns\n")
cat("wells dataset:", nrow(wells), "rows,", ncol(wells), "columns\n")

```
```{r}
# INITIAL DATA EXPLORATION AND QUALITY CHECKS

# Check for missing values in original datasets
cat("Missing values in original datasets:\n")
cat(" env:", sum(is.na(env)), "missing values\n")
cat(" wells:", sum(is.na(wells)), "missing values\n")


#First we merge the environment and wells data
# Find common variables for merging
common_vars <- intersect(names(env), names(wells))
cat("Common variables for merging:", paste(common_vars, collapse = ", "), "\n")

# Check for duplicate WELL_IDs (important for data quality)
cat("Duplicate WELL_IDs check:\n")
cat("  - env duplicates:", sum(duplicated(env$WELL_ID)), "\n")
cat("  - wells duplicates:", sum(duplicated(wells$WELL_ID)), "\n")
```
```{r}
# DATA MERGING
# Perform inner join to keep only wells with both environmental and well data
merged_data <- merge(env, wells, by = "WELL_ID", all = FALSE)

# Verify merge results
cat("Merge results:\n")
cat("  - Original env rows:", nrow(env), "\n")
cat("  - Original wells rows:", nrow(wells), "\n")
cat("  - Merged data rows:", nrow(merged_data), "\n")
cat("  - Unique wells in merged data:", length(unique(merged_data$WELL_ID)), "\n")

```
```{r}
# Clean column names (convert to lower_case, remove special characters)
merged_data <- clean_names(merged_data)
names(merged_data)
```

```{r}
# Create binary response variable using the minimum laboratory limit(pfoa_lrl): 1 = detected, 0 = not detected
merged_data$pfoa_detected <- ifelse(
  merged_data$pfoa_val >= merged_data$pfoa_lrl, 1, 0
)
#Check missing data
sum(is.na(merged_data))

#See the structure
str(merged_data)
```

```{r}
#  HANDLE PLACEHOLDER VALUES
# Replace common placeholder values with NA
placeholder_values <- c("No data")
for (placeholder in placeholder_values) {
  merged_data[merged_data == placeholder] <- NA
}

```


```{r}
# FOCUSED COLUMN SELECTION FOR PFOA-ONLY ANALYSIS
merged_data <- dplyr::select(
  merged_data,
  pfoa_val,             
  pfoa_detected,
  date,
  state,
  water_use,
  tritium_tu,
  well_depth,
  tritium_category
)

cat("Selected", ncol(merged_data), "columns for PFOA-focused analysis\n")

# structure of the merged_data
str(merged_data)
colSums(is.na(merged_data))
```

```{r}
# DATA TYPE CONVERSIONS 
library(lubridate)

# Convert character date strings to Date format (MDY)
merged_data$date <- mdy(merged_data$date)

# Define numeric columns for conversion
numeric_cols <- c(
  "pfoa_val",          
  "well_depth", 
  "tritium_tu"
)

# Safe numeric conversion function
safe_numeric_conversion <- function(x) {
  x <- trimws(as.character(x))
  x <- gsub("^<", "", x)
  x <- gsub("^>", "", x)
  suppressWarnings(as.numeric(x))
}

# Apply conversion 
existing_numeric_cols <- numeric_cols[numeric_cols %in% names(merged_data)]
merged_data[existing_numeric_cols] <- lapply(merged_data[existing_numeric_cols], safe_numeric_conversion)

#Convert character columns to factors
merged_data$pfoa_detected <- as.factor(merged_data$pfoa_detected)
merged_data$state <- as.factor(merged_data$state)
merged_data$water_use <- as.factor(merged_data$water_use)
merged_data$tritium_category <- as.factor(merged_data$tritium_category)

str(merged_data)
```

```{r}
library(forcats)
library(tidyr)

# Replace NA in numeric columns with 0 and round to 2 decimal places
merged_data <- merged_data %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(replace_na(., 0), 2)))

# Replace NA in factor columns with "Unknown" only if any NA present
factor_cols <- names(select(merged_data, where(is.factor)))
for (col in factor_cols) {
  if (any(is.na(merged_data[[col]]))) {
    merged_data[[col]] <- fct_na_value_to_level(merged_data[[col]], level = "Unknown")
  } else {
    # Just to ensure it remains factor without adding "Unknown"
    merged_data[[col]] <- factor(merged_data[[col]])
  }
}

# Drop unused factor levels across all factor columns
merged_data <- merged_data %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.factor), droplevels))

# Check structure after transformation
colSums(is.na(merged_data))
str(merged_data)

```

```{r}
merged_data <- merged_data %>%
  mutate(
    year = lubridate::year(date),
    month = lubridate::month(date, label = TRUE, abbr = FALSE)
  ) %>%
  select(-date)

```


```{r}
#See the duration of the data
unique_years <- sort(unique(merged_data$year))
unique_years

```

##Getting predictors from the tidycensus data
```{r}
library(tidycensus)
library(tidyverse)
library(sf)
library(tigris)
library(purrr)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
#census_api_key("9e916ef9e7af077626cb6474fc37fd98accc4080")
readRenviron("~/.Renviron")
```


```{r}
library(gt)

# Define years to collect
years <- 2019:2022

# Getting the necessary predictors at total level
census_vars <- c(
  total_pop = "B01003_001",           
  total_pop_poverty = "B17001_001",   
  median_income = "B19013_001",       
  below_poverty = "B17001_002",       
  unemployment = "B23025_005",        
  total_labor = "B23025_002",         
  white_pop = "B02001_002",           
  black_pop = "B02001_003",           
  hispanic_pop = "B03003_003",        
  bachelor_degree = "B15003_022",     
  master_degree = "B15003_023",
  doctorate_degree = "B15003_025",
  total_edu = "B15003_001",           
  housing_units = "B25001_001",       
  
  # Age groups from B01001 (Sex by Age) - MALES
  total_age_pop = "B01001_001",       # Total population for age calculations
  male_under_5 = "B01001_003",        # Male under 5 years
  male_5_9 = "B01001_004",            # Male 5 to 9 years  
  male_10_14 = "B01001_005",          # Male 10 to 14 years
  male_15_17 = "B01001_006",          # Male 15 to 17 years
  male_18_19 = "B01001_007",          # Male 18 and 19 years
  male_20 = "B01001_008",             # Male 20 years
  male_21 = "B01001_009",             # Male 21 years
  male_22_24 = "B01001_010",          # Male 22 to 24 years
  male_25_29 = "B01001_011",          # Male 25 to 29 years
  male_30_34 = "B01001_012",          # Male 30 to 34 years
  male_35_39 = "B01001_013",          # Male 35 to 39 years
  male_40_44 = "B01001_014",          # Male 40 to 44 years
  male_45_49 = "B01001_015",          # Male 45 to 49 years
  male_50_54 = "B01001_016",          # Male 50 to 54 years
  male_55_59 = "B01001_017",          # Male 55 to 59 years
  male_60_61 = "B01001_018",          # Male 60 and 61 years
  male_62_64 = "B01001_019",          # Male 62 to 64 years
  male_65_66 = "B01001_020",          # Male 65 and 66 years
  male_67_69 = "B01001_021",          # Male 67 to 69 years
  male_70_74 = "B01001_022",          # Male 70 to 74 years
  male_75_79 = "B01001_023",          # Male 75 to 79 years
  male_80_84 = "B01001_024",          # Male 80 to 84 years
  male_85_over = "B01001_025",        # Male 85 years and over
  
  # Age groups from B01001 (Sex by Age) - FEMALES  
  female_under_5 = "B01001_027",      # Female under 5 years
  female_5_9 = "B01001_028",          # Female 5 to 9 years
  female_10_14 = "B01001_029",        # Female 10 to 14 years
  female_15_17 = "B01001_030",        # Female 15 to 17 years
  female_18_19 = "B01001_031",        # Female 18 and 19 years
  female_20 = "B01001_032",           # Female 20 years
  female_21 = "B01001_033",           # Female 21 years
  female_22_24 = "B01001_034",        # Female 22 to 24 years
  female_25_29 = "B01001_035",        # Female 25 to 29 years
  female_30_34 = "B01001_036",        # Female 30 to 34 years
  female_35_39 = "B01001_037",        # Female 35 to 39 years
  female_40_44 = "B01001_038",        # Female 40 to 44 years
  female_45_49 = "B01001_039",        # Female 45 to 49 years
  female_50_54 = "B01001_040",        # Female 50 to 54 years
  female_55_59 = "B01001_041",        # Female 55 to 59 years
  female_60_61 = "B01001_042",        # Female 60 and 61 years
  female_62_64 = "B01001_043",        # Female 62 to 64 years
  female_65_66 = "B01001_044",        # Female 65 and 66 years
  female_67_69 = "B01001_045",        # Female 67 to 69 years
  female_70_74 = "B01001_046",        # Female 70 to 74 years
  female_75_79 = "B01001_047",        # Female 75 to 79 years
  female_80_84 = "B01001_048",        # Female 80 to 84 years
  female_85_over = "B01001_049"       # Female 85 years and over
)

# Function to get census data for a single year
get_census_year <- function(year) {
  get_acs(
    geography = "state",
    variables = census_vars,
    year = year,
    survey = "acs5",
    geometry = FALSE
  ) %>%
    mutate(year = year)
}

# Get all the years
census_data_list <- purrr::map(years, get_census_year)
census_data_all <- bind_rows(census_data_list)

# Get state geographic data with land area
state_areas <- states(cb = TRUE, year = 2022) %>%
  select(GEOID, NAME, ALAND) %>%
  st_drop_geometry()

# Reshaping the data and converting it into percentages
census_data_processed <- census_data_all %>%
  select(GEOID, NAME, variable, estimate, year) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = variable, values_from = estimate) %>%
  # Join with land area data
  left_join(state_areas, by = c("GEOID", "NAME")) %>%
  mutate(
    # Calculate age groups (combining male + female for each age group)
    children_pop = male_under_5 + female_under_5 + male_5_9 + female_5_9 + 
                   male_10_14 + female_10_14 + male_15_17 + female_15_17,  # Under 18
    
    adults_pop = male_18_19 + female_18_19 + male_20 + female_20 + male_21 + female_21 + 
                 male_22_24 + female_22_24 + male_25_29 + female_25_29 + 
                 male_30_34 + female_30_34 + male_35_39 + female_35_39 + 
                 male_40_44 + female_40_44 + male_45_49 + female_45_49 + 
                 male_50_54 + female_50_54 + male_55_59 + female_55_59 + 
                 male_60_61 + female_60_61 + male_62_64 + female_62_64,  # 18-64
    
    older_pop = male_65_66 + female_65_66 + male_67_69 + female_67_69 + 
                male_70_74 + female_70_74 + male_75_79 + female_75_79 + 
                male_80_84 + female_80_84 + male_85_over + female_85_over,  # 65+
    
    # Calculate percentages with 2 decimal places
    poverty_rate = round((below_poverty / total_pop_poverty) * 100, 2),
    unemployment_rate = round((unemployment / total_labor) * 100, 2),
    pct_white = round((white_pop / total_pop) * 100, 2),
    pct_black = round((black_pop / total_pop) * 100, 2),
    pct_hispanic = round((hispanic_pop / total_pop) * 100, 2),
    bachelor_plus_total = bachelor_degree + master_degree + doctorate_degree,
    pct_bachelor_plus = round((bachelor_plus_total / total_edu) * 100, 2),
    
    # Age group percentages
    pct_children = round((children_pop / total_age_pop) * 100, 2),
    pct_adults = round((adults_pop / total_age_pop) * 100, 2),
    pct_older = round((older_pop / total_age_pop) * 100, 2),
    
    # Convert land area from sq meters to sq miles and calculate density
    land_area_sq_miles = ALAND / 2589988.110336,  # Convert to square miles
    pop_density_per_sq_mile = round(total_pop / land_area_sq_miles, 2)
  ) %>%
  # Handle NA values that might occur in calculations
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ifelse(is.na(.) | is.infinite(.), 0, .)))

# Create a summary table for the most recent year (2022) using gt
summary_table <- census_data_processed %>%
  filter(year == 2022) %>%
  select(NAME, total_pop, median_income, poverty_rate, unemployment_rate, 
         pct_white, pct_black, pct_hispanic, pct_bachelor_plus, 
         pct_children, pct_adults, pct_older, pop_density_per_sq_mile) %>%
  arrange(desc(total_pop)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 15) %>%  # Top 15 states
  gt() %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "US State Demographics Summary (2022)"
  ) %>%
  cols_label(
    NAME = "State",
    total_pop = "Population",
    median_income = "Median Income ($)",
    poverty_rate = "Poverty Rate (%)",
    unemployment_rate = "Unemployment (%)",
    pct_white = "White (%)",
    pct_black = "Black (%)",
    pct_hispanic = "Hispanic (%)",
    pct_bachelor_plus = "Bachelor+ (%)",
    pct_children = "Under 18 (%)",
    pct_adults = "18-64 (%)",
    pct_older = "65+ (%)",
    pop_density_per_sq_mile = "Pop. Density"
  ) %>%
  fmt_number(
    columns = c(total_pop),
    decimals = 0,
    use_seps = TRUE
  ) %>%
  fmt_currency(
    columns = c(median_income),
    currency = "USD",
    decimals = 0
  ) %>%
  fmt_number(
    columns = c(poverty_rate, unemployment_rate, pct_white, pct_black, pct_hispanic, pct_bachelor_plus, pct_children, pct_adults, pct_older),
    decimals = 2
  ) %>%
  fmt_number(
    columns = c(pop_density_per_sq_mile),
    decimals = 2
  ) %>%
  tab_style(
    style = list(
      cell_text(weight = "bold")
    ),
    locations = cells_column_labels()
  ) %>%
  tab_footnote(
    footnote = "Data source: US Census Bureau, American Community Survey 5-Year Estimates",
    locations = cells_title()
  ) %>%
  opt_row_striping() %>%
  opt_table_font(font = "system-ui") %>%
  tab_options(
    table.font.size = px(12),
    heading.title.font.size = px(16),
    column_labels.font.weight = "bold",
    table.border.top.style = "hidden",
    table.border.bottom.style = "hidden"
  )

# Display the table
summary_table
```


```{r}
# Keep only selected predictors in the existing dataset as predictors we want to work with
census_data_processed <- census_data_processed %>%
  select(
    GEOID, NAME, year,
    total_pop,
    median_income,
    poverty_rate,
    unemployment_rate,
    pct_white,
    pct_black,
    pct_hispanic,
    pct_bachelor_plus,
    pct_children,
    pct_adults,
    pct_older,
    pop_density_per_sq_mile
  )
head(census_data_processed)

#Check dimensions
dim(census_data_processed)
#Check missing values
sum(is.na(census_data_processed))

```


```{r}
#Now to merge the census data with  merged_data
census_data_processed <- census_data_processed %>%
  rename(state = NAME)
# Find common variables for merging
common <- intersect(names(census_data_processed), names(merged_data))
common
full_merged <- merge(census_data_processed, merged_data, by = c("state", "year"), all = TRUE)
head(full_merged)
dim(full_merged)
```


```{r}
#Next getting laitudes and longitudes
# Load necessary libraries
library(dplyr)
library(sf)     
library(tigris) 

us_states_sf <- tigris::states(year = 2022, cb = TRUE) 
us_states_sf <- us_states_sf %>%
  filter(LSAD == "00") %>% 
  select(STATEFP, NAME)

# 2. Calculate the centroid (approximate center) for each state
state_centroids <- us_states_sf %>%
  st_centroid() %>%
  st_coordinates() %>% # Extracts X (longitude) and Y (latitude) coordinates
  as.data.frame() %>%
  rename(state_longitude = X, state_latitude = Y) %>%
  bind_cols(us_states_sf %>% select(NAME)) # Add state name back for joining

# 3. Join these centroids to our main  dataset
# Ensure the 'state' column in your 'df' matches the 'NAME' column in state_centroids (e.g., "Alabama", "California")
df_with_coords <-  full_merged %>%
   left_join(state_centroids, by = c("state" = "NAME"))
# Inspect the result
sum(is.na(df_with_coords))
dim(df_with_coords)
head(df_with_coords)

```
```{r}
#Next we finalise our groundwater well, environmental and socioeconomic 
#data by removing unnecessary columns and renaming our dataset
library(dplyr)

final_data <- df_with_coords %>%
  select(-GEOID, -geometry) %>%            # Remove unwanted columns
  rename(longitude = state_longitude,      # Rename coordinates
         latitude = state_latitude) %>%
# Reorder columns: putting pfoa_val and pfoa_detected first, then everything else 
select(pfoa_val, pfoa_detected, everything())

#Check the completeness
sum(is.na(final_data))

# Check the structure of the new dataset
str(final_data)

# Display first few rows to verify
head(final_data)



```

```{r}
# Loading the cancer dataset
cancer <- read.csv("Cancer_rates_across_states_data_only-2.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

#Clean the column names
cancer <- clean_names(cancer)

#check missing values
sum(is.na(cancer))
# Check the original data type
str(cancer)
```
```{r}
# Check the merge key
common <- intersect(names(final_data), names(cancer))
print(common) 
cancer <- cancer %>% filter(state != "US")

```

```{r}
# Perform the merge
merged <- left_join(final_data, cancer, by = "state")

# Check the result
sum(is.na(merged))
str(merged)
```


```{r}
merged_subset <- merged %>%
  select(
    pfoa_val,
    pfoa_detected,
    state,
    water_use,
    tritium_tu,
    well_depth,
    tritium_category,
    month,
    median_income,
    total_pop,
    poverty_rate,
    unemployment_rate,
    pct_white,
    pct_black,
    pct_hispanic,
    pct_bachelor_plus,
    pct_children,
    pct_adults,
    pct_older,
    pop_density_per_sq_mile,
    longitude,
    latitude,
    average_annual_count
  )
str(merged_subset)
```
```{r}
#Convert incorrect classes
merged_subset$average_annual_count <- as.numeric(merged_subset$average_annual_count)

str(merged_subset)

```


```{r}
# Step 1: Compute the mean (excluding NAs)
mean_val <- mean(merged_subset$average_annual_count, na.rm = TRUE)

# Step 2: Impute Indiana's missing values with the mean
merged_subset$average_annual_count[merged_subset$state == "Indiana"] <- mean_val

# Step 3: Convert entire column to percentage of total
merged_subset$cancer_rate <- round(
  (merged_subset$average_annual_count / merged_subset$total_pop) * 100,
  2
)


colSums(is.na(merged_subset))
str(merged_subset)
```
```{r}
merged_subset_clean <- na.omit(merged_subset)
colSums(is.na(merged_subset_clean))
dim(merged_subset_clean)
```
```{r}
# Remove total_pop and average_annual_count columns
final <- merged_subset_clean[, !(names(merged_subset_clean) %in% c("total_pop", "average_annual_count"))]

str(final)
```


```{r}
write.csv(final, "final_data_clean.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```
